 {"id":"gt-frs","title":"Polecat name pooling: Bounded reusable names","description":"Polecats reuse names from a bounded pool (50) with overflow to sequence numbers.

## Naming Scheme
- Pool: polecat-01 through polecat-50 (prefer low numbers)
- Overflow: <rigname>-<sequenceNumber> (e.g., beads-51, gastown-52)

## Design
- Witness tracks which pool names are in use
- On spawn: pick first available from pool
- If pool exhausted: use rigname-N format
- On completion: pool name returns, sequence numbers don't

## Why?
- User experience: tmux sessions survive polecat restarts
- Users stay attached, see new polecat start (like mayor respawn loop)
- Bounded resource usage for common case
- Scales beyond 50 when needed

## Implementation
- Witness maintains name allocation in beads or local state
- Tmux session runs respawn loop (like mayor)
- Name released on graceful exit or when witness detects dead session","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:28.43866-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:33:43.883229-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-g2d","title":"Mayor session cycling prompting","description":"Add session cycling section to Mayor CLAUDE.md template.

## When to Cycle

Cycle proactively when:
- Running for several hours
- Context feels crowded (losing track of earlier state)
- Major phase completed
- About to start complex new work

## Composing Handoff Notes

1. Gather information:
   town status    # Overall health
   town rigs      # Each rig state
   town inbox     # Pending messages
   bd ready       # Work items

2. Compose note with this structure:

[HANDOFF_TYPE]: mayor_cycle
[TIMESTAMP]: <current time>
[SESSION_DURATION]: <how long running>

## Active Swarms
<per-rig swarm status>

## Rig Status
<table of rig health>

## Pending Escalations
<issues needing your decision>

## In-Flight Decisions
<decisions being made>

## Recent Actions
<last 5-10 things you did>

## Delegated Work
<work sent to refineries>

## User Requests
<pending user asks>

## Next Steps
<what next session should do>

## Warnings/Notes
<critical info for next session>

3. Send handoff:
   town mail send mayor/ -s "Session Handoff" -m "<note>"

4. End session - next instance picks up from handoff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:26.188561-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:39.861022-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g2d","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.361163-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"gt-g44u","title":"Molecule Workflow Engine: Composable Crystallized Workflows","description":"# Epic: Molecule Workflow Engine

**Vision**: Molecules are crystallized, composable, nondeterministic-idempotent workflow templates. Any worker can pick up where any other worker was interrupted and continue along the molecule.

**Christmas Target**: Full molecule-based workflow engine operational by Dec 25, 2025.

## The Core Concepts

1. **Molecule**: Read-only workflow template (beads issue with type=molecule)
2. **Atom/Step**: Individual work unit with prose instructions
3. **Bond**: Dependency between steps
4. **Polymer/Derived**: Molecule composed from other molecules
5. **Instance**: Concrete beads created when molecule is attached to work

## Key Features Needed

### 1. Molecule Composition (Includes Directive)
Molecules can include other molecules:
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: gastown-polecat
Includes: mol-engineer-in-box

## Step: install-binary
Build and install the local gt binary.
Needs: submit
\`\`\`

### 2. Standard Molecules
- mol-install-go-binary: Single step to build/install gt
- mol-gastown-polecat: engineer-in-box + install-binary

### 3. Spawn Integration
\`gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <mol-id>\` creates molecule instance then starts polecat on first ready step.

### 4. Nondeterministic Idempotence
- Steps are atomic (pending → in_progress → completed)
- Any worker can pick up any ready step
- Step timeout/recovery for stuck workers

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Polecats can be spawned with mol-gastown-polecat
- [ ] Derived molecules work end-to-end
- [ ] 10+ polecat swarm completes molecule workflows
- [ ] install-go-binary step runs after successful merges","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:49:32.005023-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:49:32.005023-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-g44u.1","title":"Molecule composition: Includes directive","description":"Add support for molecule composition via the Includes directive.

## Format
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: derived-name
Includes: mol-base-molecule

## Step: additional-step
Additional instructions here.
Needs: step-from-base
\`\`\`

## Implementation
1. Add \`Includes:\` parsing to ParseMoleculeSteps()
2. Resolve included molecule by ID
3. Merge steps from included molecule
4. Allow new steps to depend on included steps
5. Support multiple includes (polymers)

## Files to modify
- internal/beads/molecule.go
- internal/beads/molecule_test.go

## Acceptance
- [ ] Parse Includes directive
- [ ] Resolve and merge included steps
- [ ] Dependencies across molecules work
- [ ] Multiple includes supported
- [ ] Tests cover composition scenarios","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.981634-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.981634-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.1","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.983662-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-g44u.2","title":"Create mol-install-go-binary molecule","description":"Create a single-step molecule for building and installing the gt binary.

## Molecule Definition
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: install-go-binary
Single step to rebuild and install the gt binary after code changes.

## Step: install
Build and install the gt binary locally.

Run from the rig directory:
\`\`\`
go build -o gt ./cmd/gt
go install ./cmd/gt
\`\`\`

Verify the installed binary is updated:
\`\`\`
which gt
gt --version  # if we have version command
\`\`\`
\`\`\`

## Implementation
1. Add to builtin_molecules.go
2. Update SeedBuiltinMolecules to include it
3. Run gt molecule seed

## Acceptance
- [ ] mol-install-go-binary exists in beads
- [ ] Can be instantiated standalone
- [ ] Can be included by other molecules","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:11.178225-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:11.178225-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.2","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:11.180129-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-g44u.3","title":"Create mol-gastown-polecat derived molecule","description":"Create the standard Gas Town polecat workflow molecule.

## Molecule Definition
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: gastown-polecat
Full workflow for Gas Town polecats including binary installation.

Includes: mol-engineer-in-box

## Step: install-binary
After merge is submitted, rebuild and install the local gt binary.
This ensures the latest code is available to all local agents.

Run from the rig directory:
\`\`\`
go build -o gt ./cmd/gt
go install ./cmd/gt
\`\`\`

Needs: submit
\`\`\`

## Why This Molecule
Every polecat that pushes to main should also rebuild the binary.
This ensures the installed gt is always current with main.

## Implementation
1. Add to builtin_molecules.go (after Includes support lands)
2. Update SeedBuiltinMolecules
3. Run gt molecule seed

## Acceptance
- [ ] mol-gastown-polecat exists
- [ ] Includes all engineer-in-box steps
- [ ] Adds install-binary step after submit
- [ ] Can be used with gt spawn --molecule","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:12.702275-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:12.702275-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.3","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:12.704439-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.3","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:31.019186-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.3","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:31.14432-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-g44u.4","title":"Step recovery: timeout and release","description":"Implement recovery mechanism for stuck molecule steps.

## Problem
When a worker dies mid-step, the step stays in_progress forever.
Need timeout/release mechanism for nondeterministic idempotence.

## Solution
1. Track step start time (claimed_at timestamp)
2. Timeout: After 30 min in_progress, step returns to pending
3. Manual release: bd release <step-id>

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Beads-level timeout
- Add claimed_at field to issues
- bd ready excludes items in_progress < 30 min
- bd ready includes items in_progress > 30 min (auto-recovery)

### Option B: Daemon-level timeout  
- Daemon watches in_progress items
- Moves back to pending after timeout

### Option C: Manual only (MVP)
- bd release <id> manually moves in_progress → pending
- Document recovery procedure
- Witness can automate for polecats

## Recommendation
Start with Option C (manual) for Christmas. Add Option A later.

## Acceptance
- [ ] bd release command works
- [ ] Stuck steps can be recovered
- [ ] Documented recovery procedure","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:15.072833-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:15.072833-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.4","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:15.07451-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-g44u.5","title":"Spawn --molecule integration","description":"Implement gt spawn --molecule flag for molecule-based polecat workflows.

Usage: gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --molecule mol-gastown-polecat

Behavior:
1. Validate molecule exists and is well-formed
2. Create molecule instance (child beads) under the issue  
3. Find first ready step(s) in the instance
4. Spawn polecat with first ready step as initial work

Implementation:
1. Add --molecule flag to spawn command
2. Call molecule.Instantiate()
3. Query ready steps from instance
4. Pass first ready step to polecat context

Files: internal/cmd/spawn.go

Acceptance:
- --molecule flag works
- Creates proper molecule instance
- Polecat starts on first ready step
- End-to-end test with actual polecat","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:24.519069-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:24.519069-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.5","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:24.521029-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.5","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:31.275526-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-gby","title":"gt handoff: Unified agent lifecycle command","description":"## Summary

Unified `gt handoff` command for ALL agent types to request lifecycle actions.

## Usage

gt handoff              # Context-aware default
gt handoff --shutdown   # Terminate, cleanup, don't restart
gt handoff --cycle      # Restart with handoff mail
gt handoff --restart    # Fresh restart, no handoff

## Context-Aware Defaults

| Agent Type | Default | Reason |
|------------|---------|--------|
| Polecat | --shutdown | Ephemeral, work is done |
| Witness | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Refinery | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Mayor | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Crew | (sends mail only) | Human-managed |

## What gt handoff Does

1. **Verify safe to stop**
   - Git state clean (no uncommitted changes)
   - Work handed off (PR exists for polecats)

2. **Send handoff mail to self** (for cycle/restart)
   - Captures current state
   - New session will read this

3. **Send lifecycle request to manager**
   - Polecats/Refinery → Witness
   - Witness/Mayor → Daemon
   - Format: mail to <manager> with action type

4. **Set state: requesting_<action>**
   - Lifecycle manager checks this before acting

5. **Wait for termination**
   - Don't self-exit - let manager kill session
   - Ensures clean handoff

## Lifecycle Request Flow

Agent                           Lifecycle Manager
  |                                    |
  | 1. gt handoff --cycle              |
  |    a. Verify git clean             |
  |    b. Send handoff mail to self    |
  |    c. Set requesting_cycle=true    |
  |    d. Send lifecycle request       |
  |------------------------------------→|
  |                                    |
  |                   2. Receive request
  |                   3. Verify state  |
  |                   4. Kill session  |
  |                   5. Start new     |
  |                      (for cycle)   |
  |                                    |
  |         New session reads handoff  |
  |         Resumes work               |

## Who Manages Whom

| Agent | Sends lifecycle request to |
|-------|---------------------------|
| Polecat | <rig>/witness |
| Refinery | <rig>/witness |
| Witness | daemon/ |
| Mayor | daemon/ |

## Implementation

1. Detect current role (polecat, witness, refinery, mayor, crew)
2. Apply context-aware default if no flag specified
3. Run pre-flight checks (git clean, work handed off)
4. Send handoff mail to self (if cycling)
5. Send lifecycle request to appropriate manager
6. Set requesting_<action> in state.json
7. Wait (manager will kill us)

## For Polecats (--shutdown)

Additional cleanup after kill:
- Witness removes worktree
- Witness deletes polecat branch
- Polecat ceases to exist

## Related Issues

- gt-99m: Daemon (handles Mayor/Witness lifecycle)
- gt-7ik: Ephemeral polecats (polecat cleanup)
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:40.806863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:18:22.35369-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.423945-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-eu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.547204-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:24.142182-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-gl2","title":"Clarify Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities","description":"Document the cleanup authority model: Witness owns ALL per-worker cleanup, Mayor never involved.

## The Rule

**Witness handles ALL per-worker cleanup. Mayor is never involved.**

## Why This Matters

1. Separation of concerns: Mayor strategic, Witness operational
2. Reduced coordination overhead: No back-and-forth for routine cleanup
3. Faster shutdown: Witness kills workers immediately upon verification
4. Cleaner escalation: Mayor only hears about problems

## What Witness Handles

- Verifying worker git state before kill
- Nudging workers to fix dirty state
- Killing worker sessions
- Updating worker state (sleep/wake)
- Logging verification results

## What Mayor Handles

- Receiving swarm complete notifications
- Deciding whether to start new swarms
- Handling escalations (stuck workers after 3 retries)
- Cross-rig coordination

## Escalation Path

Worker stuck -> Witness nudges (up to 3x) -> Witness escalates to Mayor -> Mayor decides: force kill, reassign, or human

## Anti-Patterns

DO NOT: Mayor asks Witness if worker X is clean
DO: Witness reports swarm complete, all workers verified

DO NOT: Mayor kills worker sessions directly
DO: Mayor tells Witness to abort swarm, Witness handles cleanup

DO NOT: Workers report done to Mayor
DO: Workers report to Witness, Witness aggregates and reports up","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:56.678724-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:12.068964-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gl2","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:05.929877-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"gt-u1j.8","title":"Polecat management: add, remove, list, state","description":"Polecat lifecycle: add, remove, list, state tracking.

## Data Model

```go
type Polecat struct {
    Name      string          `json:"name"`
    Rig       string          `json:"rig"`
    State     PolecatState    `json:"state"`
    ClonePath string          `json:"clone_path"`
    Branch    string          `json:"branch"`
    Issue     string          `json:"issue,omitempty"`
    CreatedAt time.Time       `json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt time.Time       `json:"updated_at"`
}

type PolecatState string
const (
    StateIdle       PolecatState = "idle"
    StateActive     PolecatState = "active"
    StateWorking    PolecatState = "working"
    StateDone       PolecatState = "done"
    StateStuck      PolecatState = "stuck"
)
```

## Interface

```go
type PolecatManager struct {
    rig *Rig
    git *Git
}

func NewPolecatManager(rig *Rig, git *Git) *PolecatManager

// Lifecycle
func (pm *PolecatManager) Add(name string) (*Polecat, error)
func (pm *PolecatManager) Remove(name string) error
func (pm *PolecatManager) List() ([]*Polecat, error)
func (pm *PolecatManager) Get(name string) (*Polecat, error)

// State
func (pm *PolecatManager) SetState(name string, state PolecatState) error
func (pm *PolecatManager) AssignIssue(name, issue string) error
func (pm *PolecatManager) ClearIssue(name string) error

// Convenience
func (pm *PolecatManager) Wake(name string) error   // idle → active
func (pm *PolecatManager) Sleep(name string) error  // active → idle
```

## Add Flow

1. Create directory: `<rig>/polecats/<name>/`
2. Clone rig repo into it (or copy from rig root)
3. Create new branch: `polecat/<name>`
4. Initialize polecat state file
5. Create mail inbox

## Remove Flow

1. Verify session not running
2. Check for uncommitted changes (warn/error)
3. Remove directory
4. Clean up state

## State Persistence

Store in `<rig>/polecats/<name>/state.json`","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:12:27.402824-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T13:37:34.912049-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T13:37:34.912049-08:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.8","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:12:27.403171-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.8","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:13:53.747126-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.8","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:13:53.831197-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-u1j.9","title":"Witness daemon: heartbeat loop, spawn ephemeral agent","description":"Background daemon for agent lifecycle and monitoring.

## Core Responsibilities

1. **Heartbeat monitoring**: Check agent health periodically
2. **Session lifecycle**: Restart agents after handoff (Witness Protection)
3. **Polecat monitoring**: Track worker progress, nudging
4. **Escalation**: Report failures to Mayor

## Session Lifecycle (Witness Protection)

The daemon enables autonomous long-running operation by cycling agent sessions:

```go
type SessionLifecycle struct {
    AgentType string  // "witness", "refinery"
    StatePath string  // path to state.json
}

func (d *Daemon) monitorSessionLifecycle(agent SessionLifecycle) {
    // 1. Detect session exit
    // 2. Check state.json for requesting_cycle: true
    // 3. If cycle requested: start new session, clear flag
    // 4. If unexpected exit: escalate to Mayor
}
```

See architecture.md Key Decision #12: Agent Session Lifecycle.

## Interface

```go
type Daemon struct {
    rig      *Rig
    polecats *PolecatManager
    sessions *SessionManager
    config   DaemonConfig
}

type DaemonConfig struct {
    HeartbeatInterval time.Duration  // default: 30s
    NudgeThreshold    int            // nudges before escalation
    MaxWorkers        int            // from rig config
}

func NewDaemon(rig *Rig) *Daemon
func (d *Daemon) Start() error
func (d *Daemon) Stop() error

// Lifecycle
func (d *Daemon) CycleSession(agentType string) error
func (d *Daemon) SpawnWorker(issueID string) error
func (d *Daemon) ShutdownWorker(polecat string) error
```

## Heartbeat Loop

Every HeartbeatInterval:
1. Check Witness session (restart if cycle requested)
2. Check Refinery session (restart if cycle requested)
3. For each active polecat: assess progress, nudge if stuck
4. Query `bd ready` for new work, spawn up to max_workers","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T17:12:29.389103-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:12.047959-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:12.047959-08:00","close_reason":"Folded into gt-99m (one daemon for all Gas Town).  {"id":"gt-u82","title":"Design: Mayor session cycling and handoff","description":"Design for Mayor session cycling and structured handoff.

## Overview

Mayor coordinates across all rigs and runs for extended periods. Needs session cycling pattern with structured handoff notes.

## Key Elements

1. Session cycling recognition (when to cycle)
2. Handoff note format (structured state capture)
3. Handoff delivery (mail to self)
4. Fresh session startup (reading and resuming)

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-g2d: Mayor session cycling prompting
- gt-sye: Mayor startup protocol prompting
- gt-vci: Mayor handoff mail template
- gt-1le: town handoff command (optional, P2)

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:03:16.125725-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:49:26.203276-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T20:16:10.772149-08:00","close_reason":"Design complete in docs/mayor-handoff-design.md. 4 subtasks created for implementation."}

 {"id":"gt-v5k","title":"Design: Failure modes and recovery","description":"Document failure modes and recovery strategies for Gas Town operations.

## Critical Failure Modes

### 1. Agent Crash Mid-Operation

**Scenario**: Polecat crashes while committing, Witness crashes while verifying

**Detection**:
- Session suddenly gone (tmux check fails)
- State shows 'working' but no session
- Heartbeat stops (for Witness)

**Recovery**:
- Doctor detects via ZombieSessionCheck
- Capture any recoverable state
- Reset agent state to 'idle'
- For Witness: auto-restart via supervisor or manual gt witness start

### 2. Git State Corruption

**Scenario**: Merge conflict, failed rebase, detached HEAD

**Detection**:
- Git commands fail
- Dirty state that won't commit
- Branch diverged from origin

**Recovery**:
- gt doctor reports git health issues
- Manual intervention recommended
- Severe cases: remove clone, re-clone

### 3. Beads Sync Conflict

**Scenario**: Two polecats modify same issue

**Detection**:
- bd sync fails with conflict
- Beads tombstone mechanism handles most cases

**Recovery**:
- Beads has last-write-wins semantics
- bd sync --force in extreme cases
- Issues may need manual dedup

### 4. Tmux Failure

**Scenario**: Tmux server crashes, socket issues

**Detection**:
- All sessions inaccessible
- "no server running" errors

**Recovery**:
- Kill any orphan processes
- tmux kill-server && tmux start-server
- All agent states reset to idle
- Re-spawn active work

### 5. Claude API Issues

**Scenario**: Rate limits, outages, context limits

**Detection**:
- Sessions hang or produce errors
- Repeated failure patterns

**Recovery**:
- Exponential backoff (handled by Claude Code)
- For context limits: session cycling (mail-to-self)
- For outages: wait and retry

### 6. Disk Full

**Scenario**: Clones, logs, or beads fill disk

**Detection**:
- Write operations fail
- git/bd commands error

**Recovery**:
- Clean up logs: rm ~/.gastown/logs/*
- Remove old polecat clones
- gt doctor --fix can clean some cruft

### 7. Network Failure

**Scenario**: Can't reach GitHub, API servers

**Detection**:
- git fetch/push fails
- Claude sessions hang

**Recovery**:
- Work continues locally
- Queue pushes for later
- Sync when connectivity restored

## Recovery Principles

1. **Fail safe**: Prefer stopping over corrupting
2. **State is recoverable**: Git and beads have recovery mechanisms
3. **Doctor heals**: gt doctor --fix handles common issues
4. **Emergency stop**: gt stop --all as last resort
5. **Human escalation**: Some failures need Overseer intervention

## Implementation

- Document each failure mode in architecture.md
- Ensure doctor checks cover detection
- Add recovery hints to error messages
- Log all failures for debugging","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:07.198289-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:28.171942-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-vc1n","title":"Tmux status line: rig color themes and worker identity display","description":"## Summary

Customize tmux status line for Gas Town workers with:
1. Per-rig configurable color themes
2. Clear worker name and role visibility

## Current Problem

- Only mayor shows in status line (and truncated)
- Can't tell which rig/worker you're looking at
- All sessions look the same

## Proposed Design

### Per-rig colors
```yaml
# In rig config or beads
theme:
  primary: '#ff6600'    # Orange for gastown
  secondary: '#333333'
  accent: '#ffcc00'
```

### Status line format
```
[gastown/Rictus] polecat | gt-70b3 | branch: polecat/Rictus
[beads/emma] crew | working | branch: main  
[mayor] coordinator | idle
```

### Components
- Rig name with rig color
- Worker name
- Role (polecat/crew/mayor/witness/refinery)
- Current issue or status
- Branch name

## Configuration

Could use pinned beads for this (see gm-w13, beads-6v2):
- `bd show <rig>-theme` returns theme config
- Stored as pinned bead, always available
- Part of 'config in beads data plane' initiative","notes":"Implementation complete. Core features: per-rig color themes, dynamic status line with issue/mail indicators, gt theme/issue commands. Ready for testing.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:58.547188-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:25:02.135073-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-vjw","title":"Swarm learning: Session cleanup missing from swarm workflow","description":"## Problem

After Enders Game swarm completed (18 issues merged), 16 polecat sessions were left running but idle. No automated cleanup occurred.

## What Should Happen

1. Witness detects polecat completed work (idle at prompt)
2. Witness verifies git state is clean
3. Witness shuts down session
4. Witness reports completion to Mayor

## GGT Components

- gt-cxx: Witness context cycling (covers self-cycling)
- gt-u1j.9: Witness daemon heartbeat loop
- gt-kmn.6: Witness swarm landing protocol

## Recommendation

Add to Witness responsibilities:
- Monitor for 'work complete' signals (DONE keyword, idle detection)
- Automated session shutdown after verification
- Swarm completion reporting to Mayor

See also: architecture.md 'Worker Cleanup (Witness-Owned)' section which describes this but it wasn't implemented in PGT.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:27:52.796587-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:37.409965-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:37.409965-08:00","close_reason":"Addressed by gt-u1j.9 (daemon heartbeat) which now includes session lifecycle management and worker shutdown. See architecture.md Key Decision #12."}
 {"id":"gt-vnp9","title":"tmux notifications: display-message too subtle, use send-keys instead","description":"## Problem

`tmux display-message` notifications are not visible enough - they appear briefly in the status bar and are easy to miss.

## Current Behavior

router.go uses:
```go
r.tmux.DisplayMessageDefault(sessionID, notification)
```

## What Works

Sending echo commands directly to the terminal:
```bash
tmux send-keys -t <session> "echo '📬 NEW MAIL from mayor'" Enter
```

## Proposed Fix

Change notification method to send visible output to the terminal, perhaps with a box/banner:
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📬 NEW MAIL from mayor
Subject: <subject>
Run: bd mail inbox
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

## Considerations

- This interrupts the terminal output (acceptable for important mail)
- Could check if Claude is mid-response and queue notification
- Or use tmux popup if available","notes":"Additional issues:
1. Enter key not sent properly when chained with send-keys
2. Need to debounce and send Enter separately
3. Correct pattern: send text, sleep briefly, then send Enter","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:35:28.542985-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:29.342381-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:29.342381-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented visible notification banner using send-keys instead of display-message"}
 {"id":"gt-w3bu","title":"gt spawn: Enter key needs debounce delay after paste","description":"## Problem

When spawning polecats via `gt spawn`, instructions are pasted into tmux but workers often sit idle at prompt because the Enter key arrives before the paste is fully processed.

## Observed Behavior

- Instructions pasted via tmux send-keys
- Enter key sent immediately after
- Worker session shows prompt with no input (paste not submitted)
- Requires manual intervention to nudge workers

## Expected Behavior

- Paste completes fully
- Enter key submits the pasted instructions
- Worker begins executing immediately

## Root Cause

Likely a race condition between paste buffer processing and keypress handling. Need either:
1. Debounce delay before sending Enter
2. Longer delay (Tmax) to ensure paste completes
3. Alternative submission mechanism

## Impact

- Swarm of 10 workers had multiple stalls at startup
- Required manual tmux send-keys to unstick them
- Defeats purpose of automated spawning

## References

- Observed during Mad Max swarm (Dec 18, 2025)
- Affects gt spawn command in internal/cmd/spawn.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:06:50.734123-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:08:22.400899-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T21:08:22.400899-08:00","close_reason":"Added debounce delay between paste and Enter in SendKeys. Default 100ms, Inject scales 100-500ms based on message size."}

 {"id":"gt-w9o","title":"/restart: Personal slash command for in-place agent restart","description":"Create ~/.claude/commands/restart.md that restarts current Gas Town agent in place.

## Detection
- Read tmux session name: gt-mayor, gt-witness-*, gt-refinery-*, gt-polecat-*
- Fallback: check GT_ROLE env var

## Behavior by role
- mayor: gt mayor restart (sends Ctrl-C, loop respawns)
- witness: gt witness restart
- refinery: gt refinery restart  
- polecat: gt polecat restart (or witness-mediated)

## Command format
Uses backticks for inline bash to detect context, then instructs Claude to run appropriate restart.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:30.043125-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:43:17.182303-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T18:43:17.182303-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented ~/.claude/commands/restart.md"}
